_doc: Ekalavya ZAR-gJSD Round 1 — Codex T+L KM-R1 design. Warm-start from iter5 best.pt (step 500, eval BPB 1.398). Tests whether ZPD-masked gJSD replaces classical KL-KD's plateau.

_notes.mechanism: ZAR-gJSD = Zone-of-Proximal-Advantage Routed generalized JSD. Per-token mask: (1) ZPD band q60-q95 of student entropy, (2) top-15% by utility=Advantage*JSD within ZPD, (3) U>0.02. Loss: 0.7*KL(p_t || m) + 0.3*KL(p_s || m) where m=0.7 p_t + 0.3 p_s.
_notes.scope: Single-anchor (SmolLM2 only) for first validation. If decisive, add Pythia with competitive routing in R2.
_notes.kill_rules: Codex R1 design: step 300 > 1.405 kill; step 800 not beating best (1.3976) kill; step 1500 not <=1.390 do not extend.
_notes.promotion_threshold: Replicated eval <= 1.385 + clearly better fixed generations
changes_from_iter5: Removed repr KD (kd_beta=0, beta decay to 0 immediately)
Tighter kill rules at each milestone
Warmup 50 steps (was 150 — faster ramp since warm-start)
Unfreeze absolute-step-equivalent: layers 4-7 at +200, layers 0-3 at +1300 (best.pt is at step 500, so equivalent to iter5's 700/1800 absolute)
